name: NewFlowCount
definition: New client-to-target flows and the primary demand denominator for rejection and reset ratios.
absence_means: No recent traffic in the selected window or metric not ingested
cardinality_footguns: Use load balancer or AZ splits only; target group is not present on the live metric

name: NewFlowCount_TCP
definition: TCP-only new client-to-target flows for TCP listeners.
absence_means: TCP listener not enabled, no TCP traffic, or metric not ingested
cardinality_footguns: Protocol-specific metric; keep out of overview unless TCP detail is needed

name: ActiveFlowCount
definition: Concurrent open client-to-target flows showing connection occupancy and long-lived load.
absence_means: No matching traffic, no active flows, or ingestion gap
cardinality_footguns: Do not sum widely across unrelated NLBs when the operational question is hotspot detection

name: ActiveFlowCount_TCP
definition: Concurrent TCP flows for TCP listeners only.
optional_context_fields: TCP listener not enabled, no TCP traffic, or metric not ingested
absence_means: Protocol-specific metric; exclude unless TCP-specific diagnostics are needed
cardinality_footguns: cloudwatch=ActiveFlowCount_TCP|tsuga=aws_networkelb_active_flow_count_tcp

name: ActiveFlowCount_TLS
definition: Concurrent TLS flows for TLS listeners only.
optional_context_fields: TLS listener not enabled in this environment or metric not ingested
absence_means: Do not assume TLS listener telemetry exists from docs alone
cardinality_footguns: cloudwatch=ActiveFlowCount_TLS|tsuga=aws_networkelb_active_flow_count_tls

name: ActiveFlowCount_UDP
definition: Concurrent UDP flows for UDP listeners only.
optional_context_fields: UDP listener not enabled in this environment or metric not ingested
absence_means: Do not assume UDP listener telemetry exists from docs alone
cardinality_footguns: cloudwatch=ActiveFlowCount_UDP|tsuga=aws_networkelb_active_flow_count_udp

name: ProcessedBytes
definition: Total bytes processed by the NLB and the main throughput numerator for bytes-per-flow analysis.
absence_means: No recent traffic or metric not ingested
cardinality_footguns: Prefer load-balancer or AZ grouping; raw target-level byte groupings are unavailable in the live catalog

name: ProcessedBytes_TCP
definition: TCP-only processed bytes for TCP listeners.
absence_means: TCP listener not enabled, no TCP traffic, or metric not ingested
cardinality_footguns: Correlated with all-flow bytes in TCP-only environments; usually a protocol-specific diagnostic

name: ProcessedPackets
definition: Total packets processed by the NLB and a packet-volume companion to byte throughput.
absence_means: No recent traffic or metric not ingested
cardinality_footguns: Packet totals can be redundant when peak packet gauges already tell the story

name: PeakBytesPerSecond
definition: Burst bytes-per-second gauge for the period and a strong signal for short throughput spikes.
optional_context_fields: No traffic during the period or metric not ingested
absence_means: Peak gauges are burst-sensitive and should be trended before alarming
cardinality_footguns: cloudwatch=PeakBytesPerSecond|tsuga=aws_networkelb_peak_bytes_per_second

name: PeakPacketsPerSecond
definition: Burst packet-rate gauge for detecting packet-heavy or small-payload traffic spikes.
optional_context_fields: No traffic during the period or metric not ingested
absence_means: Interpret with PeakBytesPerSecond to distinguish packet storms from large transfers
cardinality_footguns: cloudwatch=PeakPacketsPerSecond|tsuga=aws_networkelb_peak_packets_per_second

name: RejectedFlowCount
definition: Connections rejected by the NLB and the clearest direct customer-impact metric for acceptance failures.
absence_means: Healthy baseline or missing traffic; use ratio context before alerting
cardinality_footguns: Never aggregate away with averages; use as rate or ratio

name: PortAllocationErrorCount
definition: Backend port allocation failures that indicate immediate flow-capacity pressure.
absence_means: No errors, no traffic, or metric not ingested
cardinality_footguns: Treat sustained nonzero values as urgent; keep series counts small for readability

name: TCP_Target_Reset_Count
definition: TCP resets sent by targets and the primary target-side connection instability signal.
absence_means: No target resets, no traffic, or metric not ingested
cardinality_footguns: Target group is not available on the live metric; use load balancer or AZ splits instead

name: TCP_ELB_Reset_Count
definition: TCP resets sent by the load balancer and the main edge-side reset signal.
absence_means: No ELB resets, no traffic, or metric not ingested
cardinality_footguns: Keep separate from target resets because remediation paths differ

name: TCP_Client_Reset_Count
definition: TCP resets initiated by clients and useful for separating client aborts from edge or target failures.
absence_means: No client resets, no traffic, or metric not ingested
cardinality_footguns: Do not treat as backend failure without corroborating target or health signals

name: ClientTLSNegotiationErrorCount
definition: Failed client-side TLS negotiations on TLS listeners and an edge-specific handshake failure signal.
absence_means: TLS listener metric not present in the live Tsuga catalog for this environment
cardinality_footguns: TLS-specific metric; keep documented but gated out of dashboards

name: SecurityGroupBlockedFlowCount_Inbound_TCP
definition: Inbound TCP flows blocked by NLB security groups and a policy-specific rejection diagnostic.
absence_means: Security groups may not be attached or no blocked traffic occurred
cardinality_footguns: Feature-specific metric; useful when policy blocks are suspected

name: SecurityGroupBlockedFlowCount_Inbound_TLS
definition: TLS flows blocked inbound by NLB security groups.
absence_means: TLS-specific security-group metric not present in the live Tsuga catalog
cardinality_footguns: Feature-specific metric absent in this environment

name: SecurityGroupBlockedFlowCount_Outbound_TLS
definition: TLS flows blocked outbound by NLB security groups.
absence_means: TLS-specific security-group metric not present in the live Tsuga catalog
cardinality_footguns: Feature-specific metric absent in this environment

name: UnhealthyRoutingFlowCount
definition: Flows routed to unhealthy targets and a strong direct-impact signal when fail-open or unhealthy-routing behavior occurs.
absence_means: No unhealthy routing events, no traffic, or metric not ingested
cardinality_footguns: Interpret with host health because nonzero unhealthy routing is a severe symptom

name: HealthyHostCount
definition: Healthy targets registered behind the NLB and the primary serving-capacity denominator.
absence_means: No registered targets in scope, no data due to traffic pattern, or metric missing
cardinality_footguns: Always pair with unhealthy hosts and zonal health before concluding capacity is fine

name: UnHealthyHostCount
definition: Unhealthy targets behind the NLB and the main capacity-erosion gauge.
absence_means: No unhealthy targets, no registered targets, or metric not ingested
cardinality_footguns: Global sums can hide a single failing target group; keep groupings bounded

name: ZonalHealthStatus
definition: Zone-level health status for identifying AZ impairment and failover behavior.
absence_means: Feature not present in ingestion, no data, or wrong prefix assumption
cardinality_footguns: Interpret with host counts because zonal status alone is too coarse

name: ConsumedLCUs
definition: Network Load Balancer capacity-unit consumption used as a headroom and cost-pressure proxy.
optional_context_fields: No traffic, metric not ingested, or the selected window misses load
absence_means: Use only with traffic and failure context because LCU rise alone is not an incident
cardinality_footguns: cloudwatch=ConsumedLCUs|tsuga=aws_networkelb_consumed_lc_us

name: ConsumedLCUs_TCP
definition: TCP-specific consumed load-balancer capacity units for TCP listeners.
optional_context_fields: TCP listener not enabled, no TCP traffic, or metric not ingested
absence_means: Protocol-specific capacity metric; useful only when TCP detail matters
cardinality_footguns: live_catalog=aws_networkelb_consumed_lc_us_tcp

name: NOT_APPLICABLE
definition: AWS does not emit direct dollar-cost telemetry in the NLB metric namespace. Use `ConsumedLCUs`, `ConsumedLCUs_TCP`, `NewFlowCount`, `ActiveFlowCount`, `ProcessedBytes`, `PeakBytesPerSecond`, and `PeakPacketsPerSecond` as cost-pressure proxies.
absence_means: Not applicable because cost is derived from pricing rules rather than a direct metric
cardinality_footguns: Keep cost separate from reliability severity in dashboard framing
